title: Classroom Rules
learningGoal: Write sentences expressing rules and lack of obligation using 'must', 'must not', and 'don't have to'.
instructions:
あなたが学校の先生になったと想像してください。生徒たちのために、教室のルールを英語で3つ書いてください。(Imagine you are a teacher. Write 3 rules for your classroom in English.)
以下の条件を満たしてください。
1. 【must】を使って、絶対にしなければならないことを書く
2. 【must not】を使って、絶対にやってはいけないことを書く
3. 【don't have to】を使って、やらなくてもよいこと（自由なこと）を書く
writingPrompt: Write 3 rules for your classroom. 1: Use 'must'. 2: Use 'must not'. 3: Use 'don't have to'.
promptGoal: To actively apply the semantic differences between must, must not, and don't have to in a practical context.

wordBank.transliteration: ドント ハフ トゥ
ウェア ア ユニフォーム

referenceHints: 助動詞（must, don't have to）の後ろの動詞は必ず原形にします。
must not を mustn't と省略しても構いません。

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: You must listen to the teacher. You must not run in the classroom. You don't have to wear a uniform.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: ユー マスト リスン トゥ ザ ティーチャー。ユー マス ノット ラン イン ザ クラスルーム。ユー ドント ハフ トゥ ウェア ア ユニフォーム。
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: 先生の言うことを聞かなければなりません。教室で走ってはいけません。制服を着る必要はありません。
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect grammar and clear understanding of the difference between the three modal phrases.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: Students must do their homework. You must not use your phone. You don't have to stay after school.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: スチューデンツ マスト ドゥー ゼア ホームワーク。ユー マス ノット ユーズ ユア フォン。ユー ドント ハフ トゥ ステイ アフター スクール。
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: 生徒は宿題をしなければなりません。電話を使ってはいけません。放課後残る必要はありません。
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Very natural rules using correct sentence structure.

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the user write a correct sentence with 'must' + base verb?
Did the user write a correct sentence with 'must not' + base verb?
Did the user write a correct sentence with 'don't have to' + base verb?
Do the rules make logical sense for a classroom?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: You must [verb]. You must not [verb]. You don't have to [verb].
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Verbs following modals must be in base form. Must not confuse must not and don't have to.

criteria.name: Modal Usage
criteria.description: Successfully uses all three target modals correctly.

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! Your rules are very clear, and you used the grammar perfectly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please try again. Make sure you have three separate rules. Did you use the base verb (no -s, no -ing) after the modals?
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try. Double-check your use of 'don't have to'. Remember, it means something is optional, not prohibited.

hintText: Keep it simple! Start your sentences with 'You' or 'Students'.
commonMistakes: Writing 'You must to listen' (adding 'to' after must).
Writing 'You don't have to run' when they actually mean 'You must not run'.
